source_of_truth: #264 custom DCOIR check issue body plus #263 rule-to-risk matrix

checks.intent: Analyzer, inventory, or target skip states must not be represented as successful validation.
checks.target: PowerShell analyzer wrappers, validation harnesses, and support scripts that produce pass/fail evidence.
checks.detection: Flags scripts that assign an analyzed/skipped false state while also emitting success-like validation or process state.
checks.limitations: Static text detection cannot prove dynamic object construction; ambiguous success terms may require reviewer disposition.
false_positive_controls: Allows skip branches that throw, exit nonzero, or emit FAIL status.
Requires an explicit skip/analyzed false marker plus success-like output before reporting.
checks.failure_impact: Skipped analyzer or inventory work can be recorded as green evidence and hide unscanned PowerShell surfaces.
checks.recommended_fix: Fail closed when analyzer policy, inventory, target selection, or tool execution is incomplete.

checks.intent: Native command and script invocations must check their immediate exit state before reporting success.
checks.target: PowerShell scripts that call external executables, script shims, or Start-Process for validation work.
checks.detection: Flags external invocation lines when nearby follow-up code does not inspect $LASTEXITCODE, $?, or a captured process ExitCode.
checks.limitations: Static proximity checks do not model all control flow; reviewed suppressions may be needed around well-tested helper wrappers.
false_positive_controls: Allows immediate $LASTEXITCODE, $?, or captured ExitCode checks.
Does not report ordinary PowerShell cmdlets that are not native process boundaries.
checks.failure_impact: External commands can fail while stale success state leaves validation evidence green.
checks.recommended_fix: Capture and validate the command exit state immediately, then throw or emit a failing validation result on failure.

checks.intent: FAIL rows, reports, or fixture outputs must be connected to a failing process or validation state.
checks.target: PowerShell code that writes structured validation rows, reports, or fixture output.
checks.detection: Flags FAIL status/report literals when the script has no throw, nonzero exit, or failed validation return path.
checks.limitations: Static detection cannot always identify custom aggregation helpers that fail later outside the file.
false_positive_controls: Allows scripts that throw, exit nonzero, or return false after producing FAIL output.
Treats explicit FAIL output as intentional validation evidence requiring a fail-closed path.
checks.failure_impact: A report can contain failing evidence while the command exits successfully, misleading issue or PR readiness.
checks.recommended_fix: Tie every failing row or report state to a thrown exception, nonzero exit, or explicit failed validation summary.

checks.intent: Generated PowerShell outputs must not be accepted when source-part hashes or assembly state are stale.
checks.target: Collector source-part assembly scripts, generated-output metadata, and validation reports.
checks.detection: Flags stale generated-output markers and hash fields that explicitly record stale or drifted assembly state.
checks.limitations: This check detects recorded drift markers; independent hash recomputation remains the stronger validation.
false_positive_controls: Requires explicit stale/drift marker text in generated-output metadata.
Does not report current-hash comparison code that throws on mismatch.
checks.failure_impact: Stale generated runtime or harness output can make review evidence disagree with authoritative source parts.
checks.recommended_fix: Regenerate outputs or compare source-part hashes before accepting analyzer evidence.

checks.intent: Recursive deletion must be constrained to a resolved controlled root before wildcards are used.
checks.target: PowerShell cleanup, staging, and output-reset scripts.
checks.detection: Flags Remove-Item -Recurse calls that include wildcards or variable paths without nearby root resolution/containment checks.
checks.limitations: Static text checks cannot prove a variable was constrained in another file or helper.
false_positive_controls: Allows deletion guarded by Resolve-Path plus containment checks.
Focuses on recursive deletion because that carries the highest blast radius.
checks.failure_impact: Wildcard deletion outside a controlled root can remove unintended repository or operator files.
checks.recommended_fix: Resolve and constrain the cleanup root before deletion, avoid broad wildcards, and use ShouldProcess where practical.

checks.intent: Windows event collection must be bounded by filter, time window, or count cap.
checks.target: PowerShell collection code that calls Get-WinEvent.
checks.detection: Flags Get-WinEvent calls without FilterHashtable, FilterXPath, MaxEvents, or a bounded Select-Object -First path.
checks.limitations: Multi-line query construction and helper functions may require manual review when bounds are indirect.
false_positive_controls: Allows FilterHashtable, FilterXPath, MaxEvents, or immediate Select-Object -First caps.
checks.failure_impact: Unbounded event collection can materialize large logs, slow incident response, and make validation nondeterministic.
checks.recommended_fix: Use FilterHashtable, explicit event windows, MaxEvents, or a bounded Take/Select-Object cap.

checks.intent: Suppressions and baselines must be bound to a path, rule, fingerprint, expected match count, and justification.
checks.target: PowerShell suppression metadata, local PSScriptAnalyzer suppression blocks, and baseline construction helpers.
checks.detection: Flags wildcard path/rule suppressions, broad local suppressions, or suppression metadata lacking fingerprint/justification fields.
checks.limitations: The check is conservative around suppression-like text and may need reviewer disposition for test-only strings.
false_positive_controls: Requires suppression/baseline language before reporting missing justification fields.
checks.failure_impact: Broad suppressions can hide new analyzer findings and make baseline growth look intentional.
checks.recommended_fix: Bind suppressions to path, rule, fingerprint, expected match count, and a durable justification.

checks.intent: Catch blocks must propagate failure or return a failed validation state after logging.
checks.target: PowerShell scripts that catch exceptions in collection, validation, staging, or workflow support paths.
checks.detection: Flags catch blocks that only write/log output and do not throw, exit, return false, or set a failed validation state.
checks.limitations: Static detection cannot always prove helper calls fail closed; helper-based catch blocks may need an explicit suppression record.
false_positive_controls: Allows catch blocks that rethrow, exit nonzero, return false, or set status to FAIL.
Reports write-only catch bodies because they are the parent issue failure mode.
checks.failure_impact: Write-only catch blocks can turn real validation failures into log noise while the command exits successfully.
checks.recommended_fix: Rethrow, exit nonzero, or return a failed validation state after recording diagnostics.
